title: Transformation and Preparation of Chemically Competent Bacillus subtilis Cells
author: Igem Dusseldorf
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8fvhtn6

steps.title: For 20 ml or 50 ml PARIS-Medium mix
steps.instruction: For 20 ml or 50 ml PARIS-Medium mix: %s %s

steps.title: Inoculation of precultures
steps.instruction: Inoculation of precultures: Spread B. subtilis from Glycerol stock on LB plate and incubate over night for 37°C or over the weekend at 30°C.

steps.title: Resuspend single clones in 5 ml...
steps.instruction: Resuspend single clones in 5 ml Paris Medium (test tube) on incubator roller or plate washer with 1 ml Paris Medium (saves one day work)

steps.title: Inoculation of 2
steps.instruction: Inoculation of 2.5 ml Paris Medium (test tube) from pre-culture (wash or liquid-overnight culture) to OD 580 =0.2

steps.title: Centrifuge 1 ml pellet cells in...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 1 ml pellet cells in reaction tubes for 1 min at maximum speed. Remove the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 1 ml...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 1 ml Paris medium with 10 % (v/v) glycerol.

steps.title: Thaw aliquots at 37 °C, add...
steps.instruction: Thaw aliquots at 37 °C, add 900 µl Paris medium and 500-1000 ng plasmid DNA (test tube)

steps.title: Incubate 6 h in the incubator roller at 37°C
steps.instruction: Incubate 6 h in the incubator roller at 37°C

steps.title: Pellet cells, remove 800 µl, resuspend...
steps.instruction: Pellet cells, remove 800 µl, resuspend and plate the rest (for normal transformation on LB+antibiotic)
